data.name: ExtractQrCodeBasedOnImage
data.value:
Act as a high-precision optical scanner. Analyze the QR code in this image. Your task is to decode the embedded URL exactly as it is encoded, character by character.
First, identify the type of data (e.g., a URL). Carefully decode the string. Double-check for common URL structures (https, subdomains, parameters). Output ONLY the final decoded URL string without any conversational filler.

data.name: ExtractReceiptBasedOnHtmlContent
ExtractReceiptBasedOnImage
data.value:
Brazilian NFC-e Extraction Prompt (Decimal Version)
You are a specialized Brazilian NFC-e (Nota Fiscal de Consumidor Eletrônica) data extraction engine. Your goal is to convert a receipt image into a high-precision JSON object. 1. Image & OCR Pre-processing
The receipt may be rotated or at an angle; mentally deskew before reading. Brazilian receipts use a COMMA (,) as a decimal separator. You must convert this to a DOT (.) for the JSON output.
2. Date & Time Formatting (STRICT - Local Time)
Naive ISO-8601 Format: All dates and timestamps must be returned in ISO-8601 format WITHOUT any timezone suffix (no "Z", no "+00:00", no "-03:00"). Example: "08/03/2026 14:29:40" → "2026-03-08T14:29:40" This ensures the time is treated as "Local Time" exactly as printed on the receipt. If only a date is available, use YYYY-MM-DD.
3. Numeric & Money Extraction Rules (STRICT)
Capture First: Identify the exact string on the image (e.g., "131,67" or "0,184"). Decimal BRL Format: All currency and price values must be returned as JSON numbers (floats) with two decimal places. Example: "R$ 131,67" → 131.67 Example: "8,74" → 8.74 Example: "10,90" → 10.9 Quantities: Keep as decimal numbers with as many places as shown on the receipt. Example: "0,184" → 0.184 No Truncation: Do NOT convert to integer cents. Do NOT drop the decimals. If the receipt says 29,75, the JSON must be 29.75. Validation: If an item shows Qty: 2.0 and Unit Price: 5.49, the Total Price MUST be 10.98.
4. QR Code & Access Key Logic
QR URL: Locate the QR code. If you can visually decode it, extract the full URL (including the hash suffix after the | symbols). Fallback: If the QR is blurry, locate the 44-digit "Chave de Acesso" printed on the receipt. Reconstruction: If you only have the 44 digits, reconstruct the URL using the merchant's state (e.g., rs for Rio Grande do Sul): https://www.sefaz.[UF].gov.br/NFCE/NFCE-COM.aspx?p=[44-DIGITS]|2|1|1. Decomposition: From the 44-digit key, extract: Digits 1-2: cUF (State) Digits 3-6: AAMM (Year/Month) Digits 7-20: cnpj_digits Digits 26-34: nNF (Invoice Number)
5. Items & Tagging
For each item, extract: description_raw, product_code_raw (Código), quantity, unit, unit_price, and total_price. Assign 1–3 tags from this list: grocery, beverage, alcohol, tobacco, restaurant, pharmacy, personal_care, household, electronics, clothing, fuel, service, transport, unknown.
6. Output Format
Return ONLY a valid JSON object. No prose, no markdown blocks, no explanations. Use null for missing or unreadable fields.
json { "document_type": "nfce_receipt", "source": { "source_type": "receipt_image", "image_file": null, "ingested_at": null, "notes": [] }, "qr": { "decoded_ok": false, "url": null, "fallback_used": false, "fallback_source": null }, "merchant": { "legal_name": null, "cnpj": null, "address": { "street": null, "number": null, "neighborhood": null, "city": null, "state": null, "country": "BR" } }, "transaction": { "model": "65", "nfce_number": null, "series": null, "access_key_44": null, "access_key_decomposed": { "cUF": null, "AAMM": null, "cnpj_digits": null, "nNF": null }, "issue_datetime": null, "authorization_protocol": null, "environment": null }, "totals": { "items_count": null, "total": null, "currency": "BRL", "payment": [ { "method": null, "amount": null } ] }, "taxes": { "taxes_total_incidents_displayed": null, "ibpt_approx": { "federal": null, "state": null, "source_code": null } }, "items": [ { "line_number": null, "description_raw": null, "product_code_raw": null, "quantity": null, "unit": null, "unit_price": null, "total_price": null, "tags": ["unknown"], "confidence": { "description": 1.0, "quantity": 1.0, "prices": 1.0 } } ], "retrieval": { "fingerprints": { "merchant_cnpj_digits": null, "issue_date": null, "total_brl": null } } }

data.name: ExtractReceiptBasedOnUrlAndNavigate
ExtractReceiptBasedOnUrlInfo
data.value:
You are an information extraction and classification engine for Brazilian NFC-e ("Documento Auxiliar da Nota Fiscal de Consumidor Eletrônica") consultation pages.
Input: a single URL to an NFC-e QR/consultation page, like: {{URL}}
Task:
Open the URL and extract all available receipt data: merchant identification, address, NFC-e number/series, access key, issue datetime, authorization protocol, environment, items (description, product code, quantity, unit, unit price, total price), totals (items count, total amount), payments (method and amount), and taxes (total displayed; IBPT approx federal/state if present). Normalize and output ONLY a single JSON object matching the schema below exactly (same keys, same nesting). Use null where the page does not provide the value. Use decimal numbers with a dot. Populate: qr.decoded_ok = true and qr.url = {{URL}} if the page is accessible. source.notes must include the source URL. Tagging requirements (broad retail coverage) Add purchase_tags (array of strings) at the top level (new field) summarizing the purchase overall. For each item, set items[].tags to meaningful tags inferred from description_raw. Tags must be lowercase snake_case, short, reusable. Prefer the controlled vocabulary below, but you may create new tags when needed for non-grocery domains (tools, electronics, services, etc.). When creating new tags, keep them generic (category-level), not brand- or store-specific. If uncertain, include "unknown".
Controlled tag vocabulary (prefer these first):
groceries, food, beverage, alcohol, meat, poultry, seafood, dairy, cheese, deli_meat, bakery, sweets, snacks, pantry, canned_food, frozen, produce household, cleaning, paper_goods, kitchenware, cutlery, tableware, home_textiles, furniture, decor clothing, footwear, accessories personal_care, health, pharmacy, baby, pets electronics, appliances, computing, phone_accessories tools, hardware, construction, automotive, gardening stationery, books, toys, sports, hobbies services, digital_goods, subscription, delivery_fee, warranty, installation restaurant, fuel unknown
Tagging logic:
Assign 1–4 tags per item based on keywords and context (e.g., "martelo" → tools, hardware; "pilha" → electronics; "corte de cabelo" → services, personal_care; "taxa/entrega" → services, delivery_fee). purchase_tags should summarize the overall basket: include "nfce_receipt" always include the top 3–6 categories represented across items (by count and/or share of total value) if items span multiple domains, include multiple tags (e.g., groceries, household, clothing)
Output constraints:
Output must be valid JSON only (no markdown, no explanation). Keep original raw strings as shown on the receipt for description_raw and product_code_raw. issue_datetime must be ISO-8601 with timezone if you can infer it; otherwise null. environment should be "producao" if the page says "Ambiente de Produção"; otherwise null. access_key_decomposed: cUF = first 2 digits of the 44-digit key AAMM = digits 3–6 cnpj_digits = CNPJ digits only (14 digits) if present in the key or merchant section nNF = NFC-e number if present The items array must contain one object per line item on the receipt. Do not merge or skip items. confidence values should reflect your certainty: use 1.0 if the value was clearly stated on the page, lower values (e.g. 0.7) if inferred or ambiguous. Your entire response must be the raw JSON object only. Do not wrap it in markdown code fences (no triple backticks, no ```json), do not add any text before or after the JSON. Start your response with { and end with }.
Output schema (follow exactly):
{ "document_type": "nfce_receipt", "purchase_tags": [], "source": { "source_type": "receipt_url", "image_file": null, "ingested_at": null, "notes": [] }, "qr": { "decoded_ok": false, "url": null, "fallback_used": false, "fallback_source": null }, "merchant": { "legal_name": null, "cnpj": null, "address": { "street": null, "number": null, "neighborhood": null, "city": null, "state": null, "country": "BR" } }, "transaction": { "model": "65", "nfce_number": null, "series": null, "access_key_44": null, "access_key_decomposed": { "cUF": null, "AAMM": null, "cnpj_digits": null, "nNF": null }, "issue_datetime": null, "authorization_protocol": null, "environment": null }, "totals": { "items_count": null, "total": null, "currency": "BRL", "payment": [ { "method": null, "amount": null } ] }, "taxes": { "taxes_total_incidents_displayed": null, "ibpt_approx": { "federal": null, "state": null, "source_code": null } }, "items": [ { "line_number": null, "description_raw": null, "product_code_raw": null, "quantity": null, "unit": null, "unit_price": null, "total_price": null, "tags": ["unknown"], "confidence": { "description": 1.0, "quantity": 1.0, "prices": 1.0 } } ], "retrieval": { "fingerprints": { "merchant_cnpj_digits": null, "issue_date": null, "total_brl": null } } }
Now process this URL: {{URL}}

data.name: WhatDoYouSee
data.value: What do you see in this image?
